title: Tococa heterophylla
intro: Tococa heterophylla, also known as the "Tococa tree," is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Tococa heterophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Tococa heterophylla belongs to the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the memecylid or tarantula flower family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The Melastomataceae family consists of approximately 160 genera and over 5,000 species. Tococa heterophylla's classification is further divided into its species and varieties, showcasing its unique characteristics. The plant's classification is essential for understanding its evolution, growth, and adaptation to its environment.
